Based on the AI model's prediction,
The fact that there is no checking account, because account status is a critical inspection point for classifying bad credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [1]
The fact that there is a critical account or other existing credit, because credit history is a significant predictor in credit risk classification models, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that the loan duration is extended, because longer durations increase the likelihood of default, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.

Özdemir (2008) finds that financial variables have a stronger influence on risk classification than demographic variables, which is consistent with the findings of this study.
